reasoning: `implies_apply` is external_body bridging `p.implies(q).satisfied_by(ex)` — which evaluates a fresh closure from `TempPred::new` — to the direct modus ponens conclusion. This is an unverified trust assumption resolving the intensional closure gap.

Composing `entails_and_temp` to build `spec.entails(p.and(q).and(r))` from individual entailments is standard conjunction introduction — a correct and expected property. No new trust surface beyond what φ1 already identifies.

`spec.entails(p)` implying `spec.entails(p.and(p))` is just conjunction idempotence, a standard and desirable logical property. It's a direct instantiation of `entails_and_temp` with no new trust surface.
If `p` holds on all executions (`valid(p)`), then any spec trivially entails `p` — this is the definition of validity. The "collapse" is correct: valid properties are entailed by everything.
An unsatisfiable spec vacuously entailing anything is standard ex falso quodlibet — a fundamental property of implication, not a spec gap.
